seo.title: Building Customer Support with LlamaIndex + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with LlamaIndex + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building customer support agents with LlamaIndex enables RAG-powered responses from knowledge bases and CRM data, delivering fast, context-aware assistance. However, irrelevant retrievals, hallucinations, and policy violations can damage trust and compliance. Vex guardrails ensure response accuracy, PII safety, and consistent tone across high-stakes interactions.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with LlamaIndex
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture starts with a LlamaIndex VectorStoreIndex built from customer knowledge base documents and CRM data exports. Queries trigger retrieval from the index, augmented by metadata filters for ticket categories. The RAG chain combines retrieved context with LLM synthesis, plus tool-calling for actions like ticket creation via Zendesk API or order lookup in Stripe. Responses are routed based on sentiment analysis and escalation triggers.

Key components include chunked document ingestion with overlap to prevent boundary errors, periodic reindexing via webhooks from CRM updates, and a query engine with custom routers for multi-source retrieval. The agent handles natural language queries like 'refund my order #12345' by retrieving relevant docs, calling order lookup tools, and generating compliant responses.

This setup scales to thousands of daily queries but requires verification at the query engine output to catch retrieval failures and ensure policy adherence.

risks.name: Retrieved context irrelevant to query
risks.description: Vector search returns docs about 'billing' for refund queries, leading to wrong advice.
risks.mitigation: Vex semantic similarity checks between query and retrieved chunks before synthesis.

risks.name: Synthesizer hallucinating beyond source documents
risks.description: LLM invents refund policies not present in retrieved knowledge base.
risks.mitigation: Vex citation verification ensures all claims trace back to source documents.

risks.name: Index staleness causing outdated responses
risks.description: Policy changes in CRM not reflected in index, giving incorrect escalation instructions.
risks.mitigation: Vex freshness checks against CRM webhook timestamps and reindex triggers.

risks.name: Chunk boundary errors losing critical context
risks.description: Important refund deadline split across chunks, causing incomplete retrieval.
risks.mitigation: Vex context continuity analysis across retrieved chunk boundaries.

implementation.title: Index Construction from Knowledge Base
implementation.description: Create VectorStoreIndex from customer support docs with metadata for filtering.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader, StorageContext
from llama_index.vector_stores.chroma import ChromaVectorStore
import chromadb

db = chromadb.PersistentClient(path="./chroma_db")
chroma_collection = db.get_or_create_collection("support_docs")
vector_store = ChromaVectorStore(chroma_collection=chroma_collection)
storage_context = StorageContext.from_defaults(vector_store=vector_store)

documents = SimpleDirectoryReader("./support_docs").load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(
    documents, storage_context=storage_context
)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine(similarity_top_k=3)

implementation.title: RAG Query Engine with Tool Calling
implementation.description: Wrap query engine with tools for CRM lookup and ticket creation.
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core.tools import FunctionTool
from llama_index.llms.openai import OpenAI

llm = OpenAI(model="gpt-4")

def lookup_order(order_id: str) -> str:
    return f"Order #{order_id}: Status=shipped, Total=$49.99"

def create_ticket(customer_email: str, issue: str) -> str:
    return f"Ticket created for {customer_email}: {issue}"

order_tool = FunctionTool.from_defaults(fn=lookup_order)
ticket_tool = FunctionTool.from_defaults(fn=create_ticket)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine(
    llm=llm,
    tools=[order_tool, ticket_tool]
)

vexIntegration.title: Python Vex Verification Wrapper
vexIntegration.description: Wrap query_engine.query() with vex.verify() for accuracy and PII checks.

vexIntegration.title: TypeScript Vex Integration
vexIntegration.description: Async wrapper with Vex verification for Node.js LlamaIndex.

vexIntegration.title: Python Streaming with Vex
vexIntegration.description: Verify streaming responses chunk-by-chunk for real-time customer support.
vexIntegration.code:
import vex

async def stream_with_vex(query_str: str):
    response = query_engine.query(query_str)
    
    async for chunk in response.async_response_iter():
        verified_chunk = vex.verify_stream(
            chunk,
            checks=[
                "pii_filtering",
                "tone_consistency:professional"
            ]
        )
        if verified_chunk != vex.BLOCK:
            yield verified_chunk

# Usage
async for chunk in stream_with_vex("Help with billing #123"):
    print(chunk, end='', flush=True)
